name: Lowcoder
tagline: Open-source low-code builder for internal and customer-facing apps
metaDescription: Lowcoder is an open-source low-code platform to build internal and customer-facing apps. A self-hostable Retool, Appsmith, and Tooljet alternative.
overview: Lowcoder is an open-source low-code platform for building both internal tools and customer-facing applications. You connect to your data sources, assemble a UI from a library of more than 120 components, and share the result with colleagues or customers, all with minimal hand-written code.

It is aimed at developers and technical teams who want to ship admin panels, dashboards, and small web apps quickly without building everything from scratch. JavaScript is supported throughout, so you can transform data and control components when the visual builder is not enough.

In the low-code / no-code category, Lowcoder positions itself as a self-hostable alternative to tools like Retool, Appsmith, and Tooljet. It continues the discontinued Openblocks project and can run on the free community cloud or inside your own network behind a firewall.
features: Visual UI builder with 120+ built-in components and free-form layout
Native data connections to PostgreSQL, MongoDB, MySQL, Redis, Elasticsearch, REST APIs, SMTP, and WebSockets for realtime data
Modules and a Query Library for reusing component sets and data queries across apps
JavaScript supported everywhere to transform data and control components
Role-based access control (RBAC) for granular permissions
Self-hosting plus auto-saved, restorable history for version control

gettingStarted.intro: The fastest way to run Lowcoder is to self-host the Community Edition with Docker. You can also start instantly on the free community cloud.

steps.heading: Self-host with Docker Compose
steps.body: Download the official compose file and bring the stack up. This is the recommended setup.

steps.heading: Or run a single container
steps.body: If you prefer one command, run the Community Edition image and persist its data in a local stacks folder.

steps.heading: Open the app
steps.body: Once the container is running, open Lowcoder in your browser and create your first app. The default port is 3000.

steps.heading: Connect data and build
steps.body: Connect to any data source or API, build a responsive UI from the 120+ components, then publish and share with colleagues or customers.

useCases: Build internal admin panels and dashboards on top of an existing database
Create customer-facing apps that embed natively into a website without an iframe
Stand up a self-hosted, behind-the-firewall app builder as a Retool or Appsmith alternative
Reuse common UI blocks and data queries across several apps with modules and the Query Library
